title: quercus dumosa subsp. eudumosa
intro: Quercus dumosa subsp. eudumosa, also known as the dwarf or small-leaved evergreen oak, is a plant species that belongs to the Quercus genus of evergreen trees and shrubs. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of polyphenols and flavonoids. The health benefits of Quercus dumosa subsp. eudumosa include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The plant is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
